text: Our mission is to enable and affirm the highest standards of healthcare quality and patient safety for all. We do this by evaluating healthcare organizations and inspiring them to excel in providing safe and effective care of the highest quality and value. We are trusted by over 24,000 organizations globally to uphold standards that improve patient outcomes.

Through continual engagement for continuous improvement, we provide innovative solutions and resources to build resilient healthcare organizations around the world. Shaping the Future of Resilient Healthcare We define and advance standards that help healthcare organizations deliver safe, consistent care and build long-term preparedness in an ever-changing healthcare landscape.

We champion best practices and the right standards and measures to drive continuous improvements in patient safety and address risks across workplaces, workforces, patients, and caregivers. We bring together patients, providers, payers, and policymakers to share insights and data—advancing care quality and safety through collective expertise and global collaboration.

For healthcare organizations seeking an empowering partner to help them meet the most rigorous standards, Joint Commission brings unmatched expertise and a commitment to advancing patient and caregiver safety across the entire continuum of care.

years advancing healthcare quality for all countries served by the Joint Commission enterprise Through accreditation, certification, and a comprehensive suite of performance improvement solutions, we help healthcare organizations fulfill their mission to provide safe, high-quality care—today and into the future.

Joint Commission accreditation is the objective evaluation process that can help health care organizations measure, assess, and improve performance in order to provide safe, high quality care for their patients. Certification focuses on specific clinical programs or services that an organization provides and evaluates the quality and safety of care provided in these specialized areas.

Our portfolio of comprehensive, integrated products and services is designed to streamline operations and keep patients safe. Our team can help you build a solution tailored to the unique goals of your organization. Serving Patients and the Healthcare Community Explore the resource centers below for specific healthcare challenges.

We are committed to operating every part of our business in ways that are responsible and sustainable, while continuing to advance progress across the industry’s most pressing quality and safety challenges. Joint Commission collaborates with respected organizations like the American Heart Association to drive innovation, share expertise, and elevate standards across the healthcare landscape.

These partnerships strengthen our collective impact, helping providers deliver safer, more effective care to the communities they serve. Stay up-to-date with all the latest Joint Commission news, blog posts, webinars, and communications.

Becker’s Clinical Leadership: Joint Commission Cuts Standards by 50% in Sweeping Overhaul The overhaul underscores Joint Commission’s effort to reduce the regulatory burden on hospitals and healthcare organizations, uphold public trust, and help organizations achieve the highest level of safety and quality.

Becker's: Joint Commission, NQF align safety event reporting Joint Commission and the National Quality Forum are aligning their serious safety event reporting frameworks in an effort to reduce redundancy and ease the administrative burden on healthcare providers. Modern Healthcare: Advancing Healthcare Quality with NAHQ Dan Peres sits down with Dr.

Jonathan Perlin, president and CEO of Joint Commission, and Stephanie Mercado, CEO of the National Association for Healthcare Quality. Their discussion focuses on how the organizations’ strategic alliance will help standardized training for safety and quality professionals to enhance workforce competency and drive better patient outcomes.
Healthcare Executive: Unpaid Caregivers in Today's Care Economy To honor, support and create upward mobility for unpaid caregivers, ARCHANGELS and Joint Commission launched the Care Badge initiative.

Leveraging ARCHANGELS’ work with states, employers, health systems and local organizations, alongside Joint Commission’s focus on patient safety and quality of care across all settings, the organizations aim to help this vital population and their communities. Stay updated with the latest news, events, and updates tailored to your interests.
